steps.title: Find the linear scale factor from surface area ratio
steps.content:
For similar solids, areas scale by the square of the linear scale factor. Calculate the surface area ratio:

$$\frac{\text{Surface Area of X}}{\text{Surface Area of Y}} = \frac{58}{1,450} = \frac{1}{25}$$

Since $k^2 = \frac{1}{25}$, the linear scale factor is $k = \frac{1}{5}$.

steps.title: Apply volume scaling and sum
steps.content:
Volumes of similar solids scale by the cube of the linear scale factor. With $k = \frac{1}{5}$:

$$\text{Volume of X} = k^3 \times \text{Volume of Y} = \left(\frac{1}{5}\right)^3 \times 1,250 = \frac{1}{125} \times 1,250 = 10 \text{ cm}^3$$

The sum of the volumes is:

$$10 + 1,250 = 1,260 \text{ cm}^3$$
